text.title: Accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Accessibility methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Spec declares the conformance target (A, AA, AAA, or section-508). 'Best effort' is rejected.
rule.rationale: without a target, audits cannot pass or fail.

rule.statement: Spec lists the named tools used (axe-core, Lighthouse, screen-readers tested). 'We use a11y tools' is rejected.
Spec wires a CI gate that fails on critical / serious axe violations on a named set of routes. Self-test only is rejected.
Every a11y bug fix ships with a unit / integration regression test. Visual-only fixes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without regressions, the same violation reappears.

rule.statement: Spec assigns responsibilities across roles (designer / developer / QA) per WCAG criterion category. 'Everyone is responsible' is rejected.
Spec declares policy for third-party widgets (chat, payments, video) that ship inaccessible code. 'They're vendor responsibility' is rejected without a fallback plan.
rule.rationale: third-party widgets are the most common a11y regression source.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Accessibility under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
